message:
test(core): property-based tests for bloom filter invariants (wa-2jjg)

24 proptest properties covering:
- No false negatives (standard + counting filters)
- FP rate within theoretical bounds
- Union commutativity and element preservation
- Counting filter insert/remove roundtrip
- Counter saturation safety
- Sizing formula monotonicity
- Stats consistency and fill ratio

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-kqcm","title":"E2E suite completeness: runner --all covers all cases + stable case registry","description":"# Task: E2E suite completeness (case registry)

## Goal
Make sure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` is a comprehensive, maintained suite that:
- runs every **registered** E2E scenario (not just the ones under `wa-4vx.10.*`)
- produces stable artifacts per case
- prints a clear PASS/FAIL summary

This task exists to avoid coupling “runner quality” to “suite completeness”.

## Requirements
- Maintain a single authoritative registry of cases (names + descriptions).
- The registry must also record (at least):
  - prerequisites (e.g., requires WezTerm, requires docker, requires feature flags)
  - whether the case is part of the default `--all` run
  - a human-readable “why this case exists” note (what it protects)

- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` iterates that registry.
- Each case has:
  - deterministic steps
  - explicit assertions
  - artifacts directory
  - clear failure summary

## Cross-repo case coverage (important)
Some E2E scenarios live outside `wa-4vx.10.*` (for example setup safety, new IPC lanes, and UX overlays):
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10` (wa setup idempotency)
- `wa-4vx.2.7.3` (status_update IPC lane)
- `wa-nu4.1.6.4` (pane reservations)
- `wa-0go.7` (natural-language event descriptions)
- `wa-5em.9` (rollback visualization tests/E2E)
- `wa-dug.7` (environment detection + recommendations)
- `wa-tp4.8` (suggestion engine)
- `wa-nu4.4.3.5` (distributed E2E tests; non-default unless distributed feature enabled)
- `wa-9lh` (quick-fix suggestions E2E)
- `wa-am5` (dry-run mode E2E)
- `wa-jl5` (notification webhook delivery E2E)
- `wa-ugg` (timeline correlation E2E)
- `wa-p3i` (watch-and-notify mode E2E)

Suite completeness means the registry accounts for these cases too.
Implementation options (either is acceptable; pick the simplest):
- unify everything under `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` as runnable `--case` entries, OR
- allow registry entries to delegate to another script (adapter entry) while still producing the same artifacts contract.

## Change discipline
- Adding a new E2E case requires updating the registry.
- CI should catch:
  - “case exists but not in registry”
  - “case is in registry but not included in default suite” (unless explicitly tagged as non-default with justification)

## Registry
- This bead *is* the registry. Update the case list here when new E2E cases are added.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A developer can run `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` and trust it covers the full default suite.
- The suite remains usable even as cases evolve across phases.
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10`, `wa-4vx.2.7.3`, `wa-nu4.1.6.4`, `wa-0go.7`, `wa-5em.9`, `wa-dug.7`, and `wa-tp4.8` are represented in the case registry (directly or via adapter), with stable artifacts.


## Testing
- Registry correctness tests:
  - Add a check that enumerates available E2E cases and fails if any are missing from the registry.
  - Add a check that fails if the registry references a non-existent case.

- `--all` behavior tests:
  - Include a small dummy registry in test mode to verify:
    - ordering and selection flags
    - summary format
    - per-case artifact dir naming is stable

 {"id":"wa-kyl8","title":"Account selection policy (percent remaining + LRU) + persistence mirror in accounts table","description":"# Task: Account selection policy + persistence

## Goal
Given current usage info (from `caut`), select the best account for failover and persist enough state to make the choice:
- deterministic
- explainable
- resistant to thrashing

## Policy (v0)
- Primary: highest `percent_remaining`.
- Filter: exclude accounts below a configured threshold (avoid selecting accounts that will immediately fail).
- Tie-breaker: least-recently-used (`last_used_at` oldest wins).

## Persistence model
- Mirror current usage into an `accounts` table (history optional; can be added later).
- Store at least:
  - account identifier (stable id or email hash)
  - service (openai, later others)
  - percent_remaining
  - reset_at / next_reset_at (if available)
  - last_refreshed_at
  - last_used_at (updated only after a successful failover)

## Explainability
- Selection output should include a short explanation for logging/audit:
  - which accounts were considered
  - which were filtered and why
  - which tie-breaker applied

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - deterministic selection on fixed fixture inputs
  - threshold filtering
  - tie-breaker correctness
  - stable behavior when fields are missing (e.g., no reset time)
- Integration tests:
  - DB mirror updates are correct (insert/update)
  - `last_used_at` only updates after a “success” signal from the workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a fixed fixture of accounts + usage, selection is deterministic and stable.
- Accounts below threshold are never selected.
- `accounts.last_used_at` updates only after a successful failover path.

 {"id":"wa-l5ww","title":"Implement subscribe_output (vendored): stream pane output events as deltas","description":"# Task: Implement `subscribe_output` (vendored)

## Goal
In vendored mode, subscribe to pane output events and expose a stream of deltas that integrates with wa’s existing seq/gap model.

Polling scrollback works, but subscriptions enable:
- lower latency
- lower CPU
- better fidelity (fewer missed segments)

## Deliverables
- Vendored mux subscription wiring (connect → subscribe → receive events).
- Stream adapter that yields a typed event (proposed: `PaneDelta`).
- Cancellation/shutdown handling:
  - dropping the stream unsubscribes
  - process shutdown closes cleanly

## Backpressure & robustness
- The stream must not allow unbounded buffering.
  - bounded channel
  - drop policy or slow-consumer handling (explicitly choose and document)

- Handle reconnects/timeouts gracefully:
  - transient errors → reconnect with backoff
  - permanent errors → surface a structured error and fall back to polling (if caller chooses)

## Integration with seq/gap
- Define how subscription events map to:
  - seq numbers
  - gaps (if the stream reports dropped events)

## Testing strategy
- Offline unit tests with fixtures:
  - decode/encode subscription messages
  - simulate event stream and ensure adapter produces ordered deltas
  - simulate dropped/out-of-order events and ensure we emit a GAP or diagnostic

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can subscribe to a pane and receive deltas while a command runs.
- Slow consumer does not cause unbounded memory growth.
- Error paths are deterministic and do not panic.

 {"id":"wa-lak7","title":"wa robot workflow status: check progress of running workflows","description":"# wa robot workflow status

## Purpose
Allow agents to query the status of running or completed workflows to enable monitoring, chaining, and error handling.

## Command Interface
```bash
# Check status of a specific workflow by execution ID
wa robot workflow status <execution_id>

# Check all workflows on a pane
wa robot workflow status --pane 3

# Check all active workflows
wa robot workflow status --active

# Include step logs
wa robot workflow status <execution_id> --verbose
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "workflow.status",
  "data": {
    "execution_id": "wf-abc123",
    "workflow_name": "handle_compaction",
    "pane_id": 3,
    "trigger_event_id": 42,
    "status": "running",
    "current_step": 2,
    "total_steps": 5,
    "step_name": "wait_for_prompt",
    "elapsed_ms": 5234,
    "last_step_result": "continue",
    "started_at": 1737200000000,
    "updated_at": 1737205234000,
    "completed_at": null,
    "step_logs": [
      {
        "step_index": 0,
        "step_name": "check_context",
        "result_type": "continue",
        "started_at": 1737200000000,
        "completed_at": 1737200100000,
        "duration_ms": 100
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Status Values
- `pending` - Workflow queued but not started
- `running` - Actively executing steps
- `waiting` - Waiting for condition (pattern, idle, etc.)
- `paused` - Paused (e.g., awaiting approval)
- `completed` - Successfully finished all steps
- `failed` - Encountered error
- `aborted` - Manually aborted

## Error Cases (Stable Codes)
- E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND: No workflow execution with given ID
- E_PANE_NOT_FOUND: No such pane (when using --pane)

## Use Cases
1. **Progress monitoring**: Agent polls status while waiting for long workflow
2. **Error recovery**: Check why workflow failed before retrying
3. **Chaining**: Wait for workflow completion before starting dependent task
4. **Debugging**: Understand current step when things are slow

## Implementation Notes
- Query workflow_executions + workflow_step_log tables
- Include timing for each completed step
- Redact sensitive data in step results
- Support --verbose for full step logs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_status_running_workflow() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["status"], "running");
    assert!(output["data"]["current_step"].is_number());
    assert!(output["data"]["elapsed_ms"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_status_completed_workflow() {
    let exec_id = run_workflow_to_completion();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["status"], "completed");
    assert!(output["data"]["completed_at"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_status_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "nonexistent-id"]);
    
    assert!(!output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}

#[test]
fn test_status_by_pane() {
    let exec_id = start_workflow_on_pane(3);
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "--pane", "3"]);
    
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    let executions = output["data"]["executions"].as_array().unwrap();
    assert!(executions.iter().any(|e| e["execution_id"] == exec_id));
}

#[test]
fn test_status_verbose_includes_step_logs() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    advance_workflow_steps(2);
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id, "--verbose"]);
    
    let step_logs = output["data"]["step_logs"].as_array().unwrap();
    assert!(step_logs.len() >= 2);
    assert!(step_logs[0]["duration_ms"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_status_json_schema_validation() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-status.json");
}
```

### E2E Test
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_status.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR:-/tmp}/workflow_status.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow Status E2E ==="

# 1. Start a workflow
log "Starting test workflow..."
RUN=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction 0 --dry-run 2>&1 || true)
log "Run result: $RUN"

EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')
if [ -z "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "No execution ID returned, using mock"
    EXEC_ID="test-exec-123"
fi

# 2. Check status
log "Checking status for $EXEC_ID..."
STATUS=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID" 2>&1 || true)
log "Status: $STATUS"

# 3. Check for expected fields
if echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.ok == true' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.data.status' || { log "FAIL: no status field"; exit 1; }
    echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.data.workflow_name' || { log "FAIL: no workflow_name"; exit 1; }
    log "Status check passed"
else
    # Execution not found is expected for dry-run
    echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.error.code == "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND"' || { log "FAIL: unexpected error"; exit 1; }
    log "Got expected not-found error"
fi

# 4. Test --active flag
log "Testing --active flag..."
ACTIVE=$(wa robot workflow status --active 2>&1 || true)
log "Active: $ACTIVE"
echo "$ACTIVE" | jq -e '.ok' || { log "FAIL: active query failed"; exit 1; }

log "=== PASS: workflow_status ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status query returns accurate real-time progress
- [ ] All status values correctly represented
- [ ] Step-level detail available (name, timing)
- [ ] --pane filter works
- [ ] --active filter works
- [ ] --verbose includes step logs
- [ ] JSON validates against wa-robot-workflow-status.json schema
- [ ] Error cases return stable E_* codes
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with detailed logging

## Cross-reference
See **wa-n8cd** for comprehensive integration tests covering the full workflow lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:12:24.719859802Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.304072-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:45:46.260360981Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-lak7","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-lak7","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","title":"Define deterministic sequence + correlation model for replayable causality","description":"## Goal
Define deterministic sequence and correlation rules across ingress/egress streams so replay/search can reconstruct causality.

## Background
Raw timestamps alone are insufficient under concurrency. We need deterministic ordering semantics and correlation keys.

## Scope
- Define sequence assignment rules per pane and global merge strategy.
- Define correlation metadata (pane UUID/session/workflow/action linkage).
- Define clock-skew and race-handling behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ordering/correlation specification.
- Determinism and replay correctness test plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ordering rules are unambiguous and test-assertable.
- Replay results are stable across repeated runs on same input.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.113936-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:28:49.640338-05:00","close_reason":"Deterministic sequence + correlation model documented with replay-stable merge and test requirements","labels":["capture","ordering","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.925809Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955931Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Defines sequence domains, total merge key, deterministic event_id strategy, causality linkage rules, seq_discontinuity handling, clock-anomaly semantics, and replay determinism validation plan.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:12:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","title":"Define capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries","description":"## Goal
Specify and implement capture-stage redaction boundaries so sensitive tokens are protected without destroying search/replay utility.

## Background
A full recorder introduces privacy/security risk. Redaction policy must be explicit early, not bolted on later.

## Scope
- Define sensitive-pattern classes and redaction modes.
- Define where redaction is applied (before persist, before index, before query response).
- Define auditability for redaction actions.

## Deliverables
- Redaction policy and implementation requirements.
- Tests for deterministic redaction behavior and false-positive guardrails.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sensitive fields are consistently protected across storage/index/API layers.
- Redaction behavior is measurable and tunable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RoseCastle","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.155744-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:27:52.007381Z","close_reason":"Capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries documented with downstream-ready requirements","labels":["capture","privacy","redaction","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.987531Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Starting deterministic capture-stage redaction policy design with explicit pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, auditability requirements, and concrete test matrix mapped to downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:07:05Z"},{"id":10,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Delivered capture redaction policy contract at docs/flight-recorder/capture-redaction-policy.md and linked from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md. Policy defines deterministic pre-persist/pre-index/pre-response boundaries, required redaction metadata schema, implementation ownership by code surface, configuration/audit requirements, and full validation matrix for downstream wa-oegrb.6.5/7.6/8.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:08:47Z"},{"id":32,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","author":"RoseCastle","text":"Completed ft-side reconciliation for this bead. Verified and updated redaction policy contract in docs/flight-recorder/capture-redaction-policy.md to explicitly cover both wa/ft bead lineages and downstream ft dependents, and added a direct cross-link from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md for implementation traceability. Acceptance coverage: deterministic boundaries, protection scope, and tunable/testable redaction contract are all explicitly documented for storage/index/API layers.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:27:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","title":"Specify and validate capture backpressure/overflow behavior under swarm load","description":"## Goal
Define capture-path backpressure/failure semantics and verify behavior under overload so recorder does not destabilize core watcher operations.

## Background
Recorder capture must fail safely under stress; silent degradation is unacceptable.

## Scope
- Define queueing and overflow policy (drop/slow/spool) with rationale.
- Define telemetry and alerting conditions.
- Validate with overload and fault injection tests.

## Deliverables
- Backpressure policy doc and implementation plan.
- Overload test scenarios with expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Overload behavior is deterministic and observable.

-{"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context
The recorder must support multiple storage backends during rollout, including an immediate low-risk path and an advanced FrankenSQLite-backed path. We need a stable contract before implementation to avoid coupling capture, storage internals, and query/index pipelines.

## Scope
Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

## Deliverables
- Trait/interface boundary and data contracts (event envelope + batch semantics)
- Backend selection config model with explicit default/fallback behavior
- Failure taxonomy and retry contract (retryable vs terminal)
- ADR notes on why this interface is shaped for high-throughput append-only ingestion

## Why this matters
A narrow, explicit storage contract prevents premature lock-in and lets us ship incrementally without rewriting capture or query layers.

## Acceptance
- Interface is sufficient for both local append-log backend and FrankenSQLite backend
- Error and durability semantics are explicit and testable
- Feature flags/config behavior are documented and deterministic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhitePuma","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.201142-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:27:26.700401-05:00","close_reason":"Storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration contract documented","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.618035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.658992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Claimed by OrangeBarn. Drafting RecorderStorage contract + backend-gated config spec with append/checkpoint semantics, failure taxonomy, retry model, and default/fallback backend behavior for append_log and frankensqlite paths.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:16:13Z"},{"id":29,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed storage abstraction contract deliverable at docs/flight-recorder/storage-abstraction-backend-contract.md. Document defines RecorderStorage trait boundary (append/flush/checkpoint/health/lag), append and checkpoint data contracts, backend-gated runtime config model (append_log + frankensqlite with deterministic fallback rules), durability semantics, explicit error taxonomy and retry contract, required metrics, and shared backend contract-test requirements. Also linked this contract from docs/flight-recorder/recorder-event-schema.md for discoverability.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:20:14Z"},{"id":33,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed: normalized contract document for active ft namespace and legacy wa alias mapping in docs/flight-recorder/storage-abstraction-backend-contract.md to make downstream implementation references deterministic.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:27:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","title":"Implement ultra-fast append-only writer hot path for recorder events","description":"## Context
Flight recorder ingestion must remain ultra-low-overhead under swarm-scale burst traffic. The initial backend should be a minimal, append-only write path with predictable latency and minimal allocations.

## Scope
Implement the hot-path append writer (batched writes, bounded queues, durable flush policy) for canonical recorder events.

## Deliverables
- Append-only log writer implementation with batching and backpressure signals
- Tunable flush cadence/size thresholds with sane defaults
- Monotonic offset assignment and persisted write checkpoints
- Unit/integration tests for ordering and durability behavior

## Why this matters
This is the performance-critical spine of the recorder. If this path regresses runtime responsiveness, the feature fails regardless of search quality.

## Acceptance
- Sustains target ingest throughput without destabilizing watcher loop
- Maintains append ordering guarantees and crash-safe durability semantics
- Produces stable offsets consumable by indexers/replay tools","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMoose","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.012648Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:37:07.171774-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:44:02.569075-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","performance","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.012648Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.299584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.69883Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.754321Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution/reconciliation complete in this checkout: append-only writer hot path is implemented in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs with RecorderStorage contract implementation (AppendLogRecorderStorage), batched append path, bounded in-flight admission with QueueFull signaling, idempotent batch_id handling, deterministic offsets, persisted state/checkpoint metadata, and torn-tail valid-prefix recovery scan/truncate on startup. This pass added hardening fixes (removed ineffective OpenOptions::write with append, narrowed persist_state mutability) and validated behavior via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cargo-orangebarn cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage::tests -- --nocapture (5/5 pass) and CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cargo-orangebarn cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:32:11Z"},{"id":42,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented storage hot-path updates in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: (1) race-safe queue admission via atomic fetch_update, (2) tunable enqueued auto-flush controls (flush_interval_ms / flush_event_threshold / flush_byte_threshold) with default+validation, (3) auto-upgrade Enqueued writes to Appended when thresholds hit, (4) helper-based flush state reset/persist, and (5) new tests covering event-threshold flush, byte-threshold flush, and queue-capacity rejection. Validation: cargo fmt --check PASS, cargo check --all-targets PASS, recorder_storage tests PASS. Note: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings currently fails due large pre-existing repo-wide lint backlog unrelated to this bead.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:43:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","title":"Integrate FrankenSQLite backend adapter for concurrent durable recorder writes","description":"## Context
User intent explicitly calls out evaluating/adapting `/dp/frankensqlite` (multi-writer WAL + self-healing ideas) for durable high-concurrency logging.

## Scope
Design and implement a FrankenSQLite-backed recorder adapter behind `RecorderStorage`, including concurrency, WAL settings, and corruption/recovery policy boundaries.

## Deliverables
- Adapter implementation mapping recorder event batches to FrankenSQLite schema
- Concurrency model (writer sharding, lock strategy, or queueing) documented and benchmarked
- WAL tuning + self-healing strategy notes (what is adopted vs deferred)
- Validation tests for correctness under concurrent writer stress

## Why this matters
This is the highest-upside durability/performance path and the main mechanism for learning from frankensqlite in production-adjacent conditions.

## Acceptance
- Adapter passes contract tests from storage abstraction
- Concurrency behavior is reproducible and does not violate ordering invariants
- Recovery behavior is documented with explicit operator expectations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:45:14.796373-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:45:14.796373-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented and validated FrankenSQLite recorder storage adapter with contract + concurrent writer tests","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.34223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801271Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Validated FrankenSQLite recorder adapter implementation in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs (FrankenSqliteStorageConfig + FrankenSqliteRecorderStorage) including WAL/bootstrap, monotonic ordinals, idempotent batch commits, checkpoint monotonicity persistence, and concurrent writer coverage. Verification run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage -- --nocapture and CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core frankensqlite -- --nocapture (all relevant tests passed).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:45:00Z"},{"id":43,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented FrankenSQLite recorder backend adapter in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: added FrankenSqliteStorageConfig + FrankenSqliteRecorderStorage with WAL-mode schema/bootstrap, bounded in-flight admission, idempotent batch commits, monotonic ordinal offsets, checkpoint persistence/monotonicity, lag metrics, and health/flush integration through RecorderStorage. Added backend tests for monotonic offsets, idempotency, checkpoint persistence across reopen, and concurrent writer ordinal uniqueness. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex cargo test -p frankenterm-core frankensqlite -- --nocapture (4/4 new tests passed).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:43:57Z"}]}
